name: Oracle APEX Universal Theme Vita Enterprise Patterns
compositionRules: Use the Vita shell and title region for every full-page enterprise prototype.
Keep operational actions in the title region, report toolbar, or region header actions.
Use Cards or Metric Cards for summarized KPIs, not oversized marketing hero sections.
Use Interactive Report for read-heavy exploration and Interactive Grid for editable tabular work.
Use Faceted Search or Smart Filters when a report requires persistent business filters.
Use Page Drawer or Inline Drawer for contextual edit and approval tasks.
Use Badge, Alert, and color/status modifiers for semantic state; do not invent unrelated status colors.
Use Wizard only for genuinely sequential workflows with a known step order.

patterns.name: Enterprise Operations Dashboard
layout: t-Body-title with title, breadcrumb, and page actions
row with 3 or 4 Metric Card regions
row with primary report region and secondary activity/timeline region
optional Alert region for operational exceptions
generationRules: Use compact KPI cards with real labels and trend/status badges.
Make the main report the dominant scanning surface.
Keep charts, timeline, and alerts secondary unless the prompt makes them primary.

patterns.name: Interactive Report Workspace
layout: title region with Create and Export actions
optional filter region above or beside results
t-IRR-region with a-IRR toolbar
a-IRR-table with row actions and status badges
generationRules: Include search, Actions, Go, and reset/filter affordances when showing report controls.
Use row-level action buttons sparingly; prefer a leading action column or detail links.
Show realistic column density rather than card-only summaries.

patterns.name: Interactive Grid Editing
layout: title region with Save and Cancel actions
t-IRR-region wrapper
a-IG-gridView a-GV a-GV-dataEntryMode--cell
a-GV-table with editable cell affordances
generationRules: Use grid editing only when the user needs batch row edits.
Show selection, readonly, dirty, validation, and active-cell states when demonstrating interaction.
Keep grid toolbars compact and business-focused.

patterns.name: Master Detail Management
layout: Marquee Detail or Standard page title
primary form/detail region
tabbed or stacked child regions
related Interactive Report or Timeline region
Page Drawer for side edits
generationRules: Keep identity and status visible in the title or marquee area.
Use child report regions for assignments, transactions, documents, or activity.
Use drawer edits for secondary fields so the detail page remains stable.

patterns.name: Transaction Form
layout: title region with primary submit action
one or more t-Region form sections
t-Form-fieldContainer groups using label-above or floating label behavior
Alert region for validation or policy messages
generationRules: Use concise section regions; do not nest cards inside cards.
Expose required, error, disabled, and readonly states when relevant.
Place destructive actions away from the primary submit action.

patterns.name: Approval Workflow
layout: report or card queue
right-side drawer with request details
timeline region for history
badge statuses for pending, approved, rejected, and escalated
Approve, Reject, and Request Info buttons
generationRules: Keep decisions adjacent to the request details.
Use semantic status colors and badges rather than decorative accent colors.
Show audit trail or timeline when the workflow has history.

patterns.name: Guided Wizard
layout: wizard progress region
single active step content region
Back, Next, Cancel, and Finish actions
generationRules: Use a wizard only when each step gates the next.
Keep step count visible and action ordering predictable.
Avoid using a wizard for ordinary single-page forms.
